title: aster oolentangiensis var. poaceus
intro: Aster oolentangiensis var. poaceus, commonly known as the eastern American mountain aster or mountain ironweed, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as acting as an expectorant to help alleviate respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Aster oolentangiensis var. poaceus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
